publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-10-10 01:29:20 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Approximately 1,500 photographs and 1,000 manuscript pages document the joint acting and dancing careers of Irene and Vernon Castle (1911–1917) and Irene's life and career after Vernon's death (1918-1969).

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of manuscripts, correspondence, printed ephemera, artwork, three-dimensional artifacts and photographs related to the joint career and lives of Irene and Vernon Castle (1911–1917) and to Irene's life and career after Vernon's death in February of 1918, including some of her animal welfare activism. Approximately 1,500 photographs and 1,000 manuscript pages comprise the bulk of the collection. Personal photographs include Irene during her childhood, family and home life, Irene with her pets, and travels around the world. Professional photographs were created by a variety of photographers and studios including Ira Hill, Frank Geisler, Campbell Studio, Charlotte Fairchild, Ray Huff, the Crosbys, Baron de Meyer, and Underwood Studios. These images include fashion poses, dance poses, and photos taken on film sets. Photographs also include dance poses of Vernon Castle, images of him during WWI as a pilot and his funeral procession, images of Irene with dance partner Billy Reardon and husbands Robert E. Treman, Frederic McLaughlin and George Enzinger. Manuscript material includes clippings from magazines and newspapers, music score sheets, artwork, letters and telegrams from Vernon to Irene and other family correspondence and condolence letters on the occasion of Vernon's death. The collection also includes 3D material relating to the Castles' career, including a silver trophy given to them by Jim Europe's orchestra (James Reese Europe) and a piece of aluminum rudder from Vernon's plane and medals he acquired during WWI.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dancer, actor, and fashion icon and designer Irene Castle (1893–1969) was born in New Rochelle, New York. One of the great stars of the silent-film era, she was adept at using photography to cultivate her image and cinema to promote her endeavors in the fashion world. Castle first became famous with her dance partner and husband Vernon Castle. With Vernon and after his death in WWI, she acted in a number of films produced by Ithaca's Wharton Studio and others. In 1919 she married the prominent Ithaca businessman Robert E. Treman, and the pair lived in what is now the Sigma Chi fraternity house, called Greystone, until their separation in 1923.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Irene Castle photographs and papers, #8782. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505405]

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505405]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some letters include poems.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Carl Laemmle (regarding a film of Vernon flying), Irving Berlin, Willie Europe, and G. Hepburn Wilson, Ina Claire, Bessie McCoy Davis, Julia Marlow and Edward Sothern, Elizabeth Marbury, James Reese Europe, Noble Sissle, Edgar Selden, Marion Davis, Frank Crowninshield, among others. Also includes letter from Will Stewart, who later proposed to Irene.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes telegrams from Milton and Gladys Sills, George and Nesta Wellesley, RM Hurd (Chairman, American Defense Society), David Belasco, Gene Burk, Frank Crowninshield, Ford Dabney, Arnold Daly, Baroness Deymeyer, Louise Drew, Arthur Jimmy King, Max H. Manne, Elizabeth Marbury, Jack Russell, Stage Womens War Relief, Maurice and Florence Walton, Watch Your Step Co. and Bessie Pardue, and Clifton Webb among many others.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from military personnel, including Noel C. Sampson (Commander of Vernon's squadron) and a letter from Johnnie Coats describing the accident, among others.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rosswill discusses sending Vernon's personal effects, as well as Jeffrey the monkey.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dodd describes photographs of the plane crash and pieces that were saved. The photographs that he describes are in the last pages of Vernon's WWI photo album.

did.unittitle: Condolence letters from close family and friends

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally housed in wooden memorial box.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from Vernon's father, sisters, and half-brother. Several discuss Irene's wishes to have Vernon cremated.

did.unittitle: Irene's hand-written note about Vernon's birth and death

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

did.unittitle: Irene's hand-written prayer worn by Vernon during dangerous flights or battle.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vernon describes wearing this prayer in a letter from June 4, 1916.

did.unittitle: Prayer for Vernon from mother-in-law Elroy Foote

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed.

did.unittitle: "My Prayer" by Irene in memory of Vernon

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505413]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dedicated to the memory of Vernon Castle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dedicated to the memory of Vernon Castle. Includes clipping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from two fifteen-year-old "groupies", a poem from two girls asking for clothes,

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505397]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation to a reception for the Prince of Wales hosted by Philip Sassoon (1923), unsigned letter from long-time suitor William Stewart (1923), two letters from Robert Tremen discussing new family but lingering love for Irene, and letter from George Enzinger, 1933, about the Ben Bernie radio show.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505397]

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924128505397]

did.unittitle: Subseries IIa. Bound Volumes by Castle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These albums were created by Castle circa 1920 after her second marriage to Robert Treman of Ithaca, NY. Volumes 1-3 are stamped on the cover "IRENE CASTLE TREMAN." Most of the photographs in these albums were taken between 1913-1918, when she and Vernon were at the peak of their fame (although no photos of Vernon are included). Single photos are pasted on one side only. Occasionally, Castle added explanatory text or clippings from magazines to the pages, which provide some context for the images. When planning her autobiography, "Castles in the Air", Castle apparently removed some pages of images from the albums in order to submit them to the publisher for consideration. She added paper tags with typed explanatory captions to the bottom of many of those pages, though many detached over time. Loose pages from these albums are housed together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume begins with pictures of Castle's childhood and youth; many are of her early career success and her "Dutch cap" phase (1912-1914). Also includes a few of Castle's home in New York City, as well as dresses worn in the musicals "The Hen Pecks," "The Sunshine Girl," and Watch Your Step." Later portraits and poses with bobbed hair and "Castle band."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume includes art shots of Castle posing with a ball and with a spotted borzoi dog; a series of Castle at home in Manhasset, Long Island including outdoor shots; series of photographs from "Patria"; Castle's costumes for the Broadway show, "Miss 1917"; well-known portraits of Castle, including a series by Frank Geisler, and many photographs of Castle with her dogs or monkeys.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume includes Castle's "black dress, large white collar" look; her Egyptian inspired gown and beaded headdress; several stills from silent films; a "Canadian Mountie" costume and another antebellum costume with doctor bag; her Nun inspired and military inspired outfits; a series of war tableaux staged by Ben Ali Haggin and shot by photographer Charlotte Fairchild; images at Manhasset; "Patria" film stills; a series of photographs in mourning wear; four images of Castle at the "Victory Ball" in Paris wearing Persian boy outfit by Ben Ali Haggen, as well as some miscellaneous photographs.

did.unittitle: Loose pages with photographs removed from bound albums, with captions by Castle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 55 professional photographs by Ira Hill, Campbell studios, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 42 professional photographs by Ira Hill, Campbell Studios, Charlotte Fairchild, and others. Fashion shots and dancing poses, mostly for "Dances and Fashions of 1923", including the butterfly costume.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 professional photographs by Ray Huff, Ira Hill, the Crosbys, Underwood studios, and others. Many photographs are from "Dances and Fashions of 1923". Also includes photographs with dance partner Billy Reardon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album includes one photograph of Castle's sister Elroy and children, and fashion and performance photographs. Item level description available upon request.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional photograph by various photographers including Cornish and Baker (Kansas City) Ray Huff (Chicago), Ira Hill, the Crosbys (Cleveland).

did.unittitle: Professional photograph album documenting silent filmmaking, mainly "The Firing Line"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The album begins with photographs of two dogs, as well as several photographs of Robert Treman and their Ithaca house. Most of the photographs in this album document the making of the 1919 feature, The Firing Line, the first and most successful film Castle made for Famous Players-Lasky/Paramount. The Firing Line was primarily shot in Miami Beach with some of the scenes shot in Fort Lee, New Jersey; the final scenes were filmed in the Adirondacks while she was on her honeymoon with Robert E. Treman. The album begins with photographs of dogs and Vernon's german shepherd, as well as several photographs of Robert Treman and their Ithaca house. There are shots of the directors, cinematographers, crew, and other actors, including leading men, both on the set and off. There are scenes of wildlife and several photographs of Seminole Indian men wearing characteristic "patchwork" garments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional photos documenting some of Irene's experiences while in Hollywood, California, serving as a consultant to the film, "The Story of Vernon and Irene Castle," then in production. Four show her with Ginger Rogers, who played her in the film, and several with a Hollywood costume designer. One series shows her about to drop leaflets from a plane, in support of a proposed animal cruelty law.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIc. Vernon and Irene photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of Irene in the "Gavotte" costume (image used for Castle's NYT obituary)

did.unittitle: Vernon Castle and Irene dancing in "Sunshine Girl"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Vernon and Irene at their home in New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Vernon and Irene at home in Manhasset with Tell and Kiki, riding horces, in uniform during World War I, with the Belgian car Minerva, winning a three-legged race on the SS Minnetonka, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two photographs from the same shoot of Vernon and Irene dancing, with Irene in a Bendel dress.

did.unittitle: Irene, Vernon, and Annie Foote watching indigenous dancers during the Whirlwind Tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Vernon and Irene in 1913 "The Sunshine Girl" and photographs from October, 1915.

did.unittitle: Large tan leather album "Portraits of Mr. and Mrs. Vernon Castle"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thirteen studio portraits of Irene and six of Vernon; one of Vernon was cutout and is missing. These are their earliest professional photographs.

did.unittitle: Subseries IId. Irene Castle Professional Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs from a series called "The Feather Dancer" circa 1921; some images that may be related to Dances and Fashions of 1923; two photographs from Palm Beach, 1928; and several miscellaneous fashion images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Otto Kahn photograph in white fur coat; and other miscellaneous fashion images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photo in Hollywood 1938, while filming The Story of Vernon and Irene Castle, seated with another woman, possibly Ginger Rogers; photo with Claire Windsor; two different Lucile dresses, and one by Sarony of London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes four photos of Irene in Paul Poiret of Paris "bolero" outfit worn in documentary "Vernon and Irene Castle Before the Camera" and in the book Modern Dancing; a dance outfit post 1914; and a Chicago photo, 1913.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a series of photographs of Irene in a skirt and blouse with wings, October 1916, Patria; Irene in "bellhop" outfit, also Patria; and Irenr in laced bodice dress with sharktooth hem and white fuzzy broad brimmed hat "August 1916."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Irene right before marriage to Vernon Castle; a 1923 photograph in front of curtains; "Scoth Cap" Lucile suit and dog, 1914; Lucile dress with geometric sleeves, circa 1914-1916; and a 1913 Chicago photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the "Watch Your Step" polka dress; a view of Irene diving from rocks (possibly for a move); and "Miss 1917" in chinchilla coat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two photographs of Irene in velvet suit and hat, 1915; photo in full satin dress and bonnet, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes signed photograph of Irene dancing at World's Fair, July 29, 1939; snapshot at Miami Beach, 1921, published in "Town and Country."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GET TAG Contains photographs of Irene in Molyneaux "spider web" gown (1921-1923); a seated portrait by Victor Georg of Chicago (1915); an annotaged portrait of the "Castle bob" (1915); photographs of Irene in a black and white Lucile dress (1915).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of Irene in a fur-trimmed suit, annotation tag identifies "Martin and Martin shoes" and colors. Irene annotated the reverse with a longer description of the outfit, and asks "Lucile dress?"(1914). Also contains photograhs of Irene wearing a wig and dance gown (1921-1922)

did.unittitle: Irene in dress with white egret feather fan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on back: Sir Phillip Sassoon opened a new ballroom in his house…Billy Reardon and I danced…and I had this dress made of white satin with black, white and silver ermine tails made of beads. It was during our brief appearance at the Embassy Club (Note have invitation tothis event-tiny envelope)

did.unittitle: Three photos of Irene by Baron De Meyer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two matted photos by Baron De Meyer, circa 1921-1923. One of Irene is annotated by her and identifies her garment as a "Peggy Hoyt dress of real shadow lace." The other shows Irene dancing with Billy Reardon, her professional partner, 1921-1923. The third image is of Irene in a Russian dress, 1914.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purchased in Europe 1912, the outfit her first widely copied "signature look." She wore the cap when she and Vernon appeared in the Broadway musical comedy "The Sunshine Girl" in 1913. See related images in box 19.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption reads " My very newest costume: royal Flying Corps uniform with all the emblems and buttons that I brought from England. The cap is Mr. Castle's own and has seen two months of active service at the front. The dog, Tell von Flugelrade, is Mr. Castle's favorite pet that I am keeping for him until his return and incidentally is the best trained dog in America and has won everywhere as such for the past two years."

did.unittitle: Irene in gingham dress by the ocean

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These are stills from a silent film. Two possibilities are "The Girl from Bohemia" ant "The Amateur Wife." Irene was an expert diver and was often directed to swim and dive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A mix of professional and personal photographs. Some were taken in Florida. Includes photograph of Irene playing tennis with daughter Barbara, in or around Lake Forest, Illinois.

did.unittitle: Postcards with images of Castle in various garments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sleeved promotional postcards, some fashion and some performance, including "Watch Your Step," "Patria" and "Dances and Fashions of 1923." Includes envelope with note from Kenneth Godfrey (Castle gave him the postcards.)

did.unittitle: Torn out album page with Irene figure skating in Ithaca and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Torn out album page with Irene figure skating in Ithaca, Robert Treman skating, Barbara on back and photograph of monkeys

did.unittitle: Portrait of Irene in mourning clothers
Irene on a dock in riding clothes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This photograph probably relates to the film Patria and could have been taken in Ithaca, New York. It was published in an article about women pilots circa 1921.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harris and Ewing images of Irene dressed as a Russian character, with large muff; and other photos likely associated with "Dances and Fashions of 1923."

did.unittitle: Irene in Max Weldy's butterfly costume

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This costume was worn in a skit called "The Foolish Butterfly" in "Dances and Fashions of 1923." One has annotation card noting "Objected to in Boston because I did not wear tights."

did.unittitle: Castle in Paris in ermine wrap
Matted portraits of Castle by C. Smith Gardner
Images of Castle in various garments
Images of Castle in various garments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images by Ray Huff, Castle in Egyptian-style outfit, and portrait by Ira Hill of Castle with "Greek Key" outfit.

did.unittitle: Images of Castle in various garments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits by Apeda and Strauss Peyton, and Harris and Ewing photographs of Castle in black outfit.

did.unittitle: Images of Castle in various garments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including nun-inspired dress with "gorget"; advertising card;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two Pathe advertising sheets.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIf. The Whirl of Life film stills
Film stills from "The Whirl of Life"
Series III. Personal and Family Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes travel photographs, many photographs of Castle's daughter Barbara, Castle with circus animals, beach scenes, and family photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of family members, travel, Castle modeling clothing and playing various sports, and a series of 18 shots of captive gorillas. Includes an envelope of tiny, high-quality images of East African pastoralists and scenery by unknown photographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of life with Robert Tremen, scenes around London and France, Castle with her dogs and horses, German Shepherd "Tell", and family members. Item level description available upon request.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inside caption of printed title: "Cayuga Heights Ithaca, New York." Includes photographs from Ithaca and life with Robert Tremen, the making of silent film(s), soldiers firing at targets, a photograph of Walter Ash, and travel photographs and some unidentified individuals. There is a loose fan letter in this album, seemingly a postcard of a woman holding a portrait of Castle that was used in publicity for her film "The Firing Line," and asking for an autograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed photographs of Vernon Castle's sister.

did.unittitle: Irene Castle as a child and a teenager

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beechmont House was where Castle was born.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captioned "Walter Ash who accompanied us to Europe," the Foote family servant who accompanied Vernon and Irene to France in 1912 and kept them alive. Probably taken at Greystone.

did.unittitle: Photograph of que wating to see Irene at the Capitol Theatre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captioned "Castles by the Sea", featured in The Whirl of Life.

did.unittitle: Photograph of a bronze sculpture of Irene by Grace Neal
Snapshot, at table at the NYC ball with Ritchie Watson, "Evie" Maxwell and unknown woman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: Fire Island with Ted Thaxter 1940; Fire Island with husband George Enzinger circa 1950; Modeling the "Watch Your Step" dress at Fire Island circa 1950; Modeling on a beach for a calendar, 1954; photo in Chicago, plus 2 Christmas Cards from Arkansas (Destiny Farm); Irene and dog tippy in Bermuda, 1964; Irene and deer in London circa 1964; Irene in daughter Barbara's back yard, several annotated, circa 1950s; In boat with Will Stewart of NYC; unknown man, at 1964 ball; in dress she designed to wear in Florida play, 1950s; annotated photographs from later in Irene's life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two (including one in a mat jacket) are of Irene and her Saddlebred, "Lightnin,'"one dated fall of 1917. Irene had this horse while married to Robert Tremen in Ithaca and often showed him in regional horse shows. The other two are from Lake Forest, Illinois during the 1920s/early 30s where Irene rode hunters and jumpers. The one of her jumping was published in Town and Country in October, 1928. The other, in which she has her arm in a sling, shows her standing next to Noble B. Judah, Chicago lawyer and Ambassador to Cuba, 1927-29, before or after a fox hunt. Judah was a neighbor in Lake Forest, Illinois. In the early 1930s, Irene and Judah were both injured, he seriously, during a hunt chase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two taken in Florida,one in 1935; One in an evening dress with a Saint Bernard probably related to one of her "pooch balls" to raise funds for her animal shelter, Orphans of the Storm; and others.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Irene, Frederic McLaughlin, and Colonel Ivanko

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo of Irene and Frederic McLaughlin at a Chicago Blackhawks hockey game-McLaughlin was the founding owner of that team. They are with a Colonel Ivanenko.

did.unittitle: Irene with Barbara about to award trophies at a polo match
Hand drawn colored portrait of young Irene Castle, in frame

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 150 small black and white photographs, captioned by Vernon. Included in this album are: images of Manhasset, NY, one depicting Irene on horse "Minto" while filming "The Whirl of Life."; Vernon's life as Royal Flying Corps pilot during WWI, some in France, most in Camp Mohawk, Canada and Camp Benbrook, Texas; planes that Vernon flew, as well as friends such as Johnny Coats, Eardley Wilmot and Gewn Wilmot; images of plane crashes; visits from Irene Castle in England and Canada, and in Englewood, New Jersey; family members, including Coralie and husband Lawrence Grossmith, Elroy and her children; Vernon's German Shepherd "Tell" and his monkey "Jeffrey"; Gladqyn MacDougal; Vernon's cars, including Minerva decorated by Elsie de Wolfe, a Stutz Bearcat, and the Marmon. The last pages of this album contain photographs of the plane crash that killed Vernon. These photographs were sent by Victor Dodd, and described in his letter (BOX 1) from April, 1918.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Vernon during WWI, including two of a crash in Canada that killed his student, and photographs of Venon with planes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two snapshots of Canada plane crashes (not Vernon's), and aerial landscapes of France taken by Vernon in a plane.

did.unittitle: Vernon Castle, plane crash, and funeral

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two black cardstock album sheets with three photos on each, and captioned, given to Irene Castle by a fellow soldier, possibly "Dodd," Vernon's valet. Images show Vernon with his monkey in Fort Worth Camp Benbrook, the plane in which he died, and three photographs of his "funeral" procession in Fort Worth Texas.

did.unittitle: Vernon Castle's pencil drawing of Irene in soldier uniform

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of pony "Blackie" as well as polo players.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits on cardstock.

did.unittitle: Aluminum throttle knob from the plane Vernon was piloting when he died

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent to Irene by one of Vernon's friends.

did.unittitle: Medal from the New York World's Fair
Badge and ribbon for "Official" at 1939 Harvest Moon Ball

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Awarded by the French government in 1917 for distinguished heroism in combat as a fighter pilot during the Battle of the Somme, WW1

did.unittitle: Tiny silver swimming trophy won by young Irene Foote
Red, white, and blue ribbons from Vernon's casket
Token of Appreciation from the Tango Court, Europe's Orchestra
did.physdesc: Sterling silver cup with deer antler handles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented to Mr. and Mrs. Vernon Castle.

did.unittitle: Wooden box with a plaque reading "In Memory of my Beloved Husband Vernon Castle"
"Ford Dabney and the Colored Musicians" banner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Silk banner with paint writing, given at the death of Vernon Castle.

did.unittitle: Clippings about Irene throughout her life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wide range of clippings. Some are article pages, others images from publications and few are identified or dated. Highlights include first page of 1913 magazine article "Artists of the Tango" and a January 24, 1916 New York Herald article "Castles Dance at Hippodrome" which was the first of two farewell performances before Vernon joined active service in England that set new attendance records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a full page from Vanity Fair and a clipping from "The Sunshine Girl."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Corticelli silk brochure and several images cut from publications.

did.unittitle: Loose single sheet advertisements for Pathé films
Dance Magazine with Irene on the cover
"Are we going dance mad?" article by P.G. Wodehouse
Clipping "Mrs. VC of America" wears riding habit
Kansas City Star clipping, sketch of Castle in costumes
"Watch Your Step" clipping in International News Service
Chicago Examiner clipping, "Thousands Throng to see Castles in Dances"
New York Evening Journal clipping, "Do You Want to Dance the One Step?" How to photos, diagrams, instructions
Springfield Republic clipping, "Mrs. Vernon Castle with her Pet Monkey"
The World Magazine, "Teaching Dancing at a Dollar a Minute"
The Theater Magazine Advertiser with image of Irene on cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper and magazine clippings from throughout Castle's life.

did.unittitle: Signed first printing of "Castles in the Air", by Irene Castle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Castle Polka", a cancelled check, and a letter to Mr. Bok, editor of Ladie's Home Journal, defending the benefits of dancing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings of sketches from his letters to Irene while he was in active military service in Europe, 1916-17, and pen and ink of Irene in costume with muff.

did.unittitle: Vernon Castle's pencil sketch of woman in elaborate costume and crown
Vernon Castle's pencil sketch of nude woman
Vernon Castle's pencil sketch of Black caricature
Printed pamphlet "The Yank Speaks" by Elsie Janis-inscribed with a message to Irene
Souvenir Program First American Tour Mr. and Mrs. Vernon Castle
Typescript of "To the Vernon Castles, a Mild Complaint" by JS, sketches by Thelma Cudlipp
A version of "To the Vernon Castles, a Mild Complaint", as published in Vanity Fair
Commercially printed score, "Castle Lame Duck Waltz
Commercially printed score, "Half and Half, a Castle Creation"
Commercially printed score, "Castle Society Dance Folio for Piano"
Commercially printed scoresheet, "As Time Goes By"
Commercially printed scoresheet for Hupfeld's "Let's Put Out the Lights and Go to Sleep" signed by him on the cover: "To my Auntie Irene-God Bless her! "Dodo"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by Hupfeld on the cover: "To my Auntie Irene-God Bless her! "Dodo"

did.unittitle: Commercially printed scores for songs "Free," "It Isn't Fair," "I'll Follow You," and "Lazy Levee Loungers the feature of The Greek Gaieties"
Sixteen typed sheets, some with lists of songs and others with song lyrics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely handwritten by Irving Berlin, for his song, "The Syncopated Walk," which he wrote for his first Broadway Musical, "Watch Your Step," which was essentially created as a star vehicle for the Castles.

did.unittitle: Handwritten scoresheets by Frank E. Tours

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten scores by Herman Hupfeld.

did.unittitle: Handwritten score sheet for song "Soldier Boy" written for Vernon Castle by Ford Dabney
Handwritten score by Herman Hupfeld, "No More Sweethearts for Me!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely handwritten by Irving Berlin, for his song, "The Syncopated Walk," which he wrote for his first Broadway Musical, "Watch Your Step," which was essentially created as a star vehicle for the Castles (1915). Examples of his handwriting from that era suggest a match

did.unittitle: Handwritten scoresheets by Frank E. Tours

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten scores by Herman Hupfeld

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten for Vernon Castle by Ford Dabney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten score by Herman Hupfeld

did.unittitle: Handwritten scoresheets for "Someone" and an unidentified piece
